posts.name: John Glas
posts.cooked:
• 2020 Domaine Diochon Moulin-à-Vent Vieilles Vignes - France, Burgundy, Beaujolais, Moulin-à-Vent (3/24/2022)
    Day 1: Well certainly not a very good start on this wine. Thin, strawberry, sour cherry and floral notes. Not bad but not even close to the pro review. Will revisit on day 2/3. 87 points
    Day 2: Still not showing much and very disappointed. Dust, muted fruits, earth and floral note. Super short finish and lacks acidity. This is one of the more disappointing wines of the year. 85 points
    Day 3: Again, broken record 3 day wine approach works again. First two days nothing and tight. Day 3 decanted a few hours and Vinturi in full effect. More rustic and what I love about the better Gamay wines. Bright red berries, earth, rose petals, chalk, stone and forest floor. Medium plus finish and nice lingering flavors. Good acidity. Will for sure try this on day 4. Really can’t understand how anyone could pop and pour and think this was great. 91 points
    Day 4: Not as good as day 3 but better than day 1 and 2. Floral, dust, earth, underbrush and minerals. 89 points
    Recommendation: I would rather buy a bottle of the 2016 Cristom Mt. Jefferson for the same price. (91 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

2020 dichon moulin.JPG3264×2448 1.07 MB

posts.name: Zachary Ross
posts.cooked:
Interesting, I have been sizing up some Moulin-à-Vents and of course Diochon is among them.

What was the pro review?

Anyone tried the Rottiers wines recently? I picked up some 2018 Rottiers Moulin-à-Vent Champ de Cour but have never had the wines.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Sad to hear it. It showed very well when I tried it at the estate last year. It’s generally one of the more consistent wines of the AOC, the core of the blend deriving from this parcel, which is one of the most enviably situated in the AOC: Google Maps

No. Not many people decant in the Beaujolais…!

This wine sure needed it. Of course many factors go into the wine once it leaves the winery. No signs off an flaws and shipped during decant weather. I might have to get another bottle and age it.

The Rottiers wines are getting very interesting! Definitely worth trying.

posts.name: Zachary Ross
posts.cooked:
Anyone tried the Rottiers wines recently? I picked up some 2018 Rottiers Moulin-à-Vent Champ de Cour but have never had the wines.

The Rottiers wines are getting very interesting! Definitely worth trying.

Thank you. I also bought some 2020 Rottiers MaV Dernier Souffle. Any thoughts on these two cuvees that you’d care to share? Also, by “getting interesting,” what do you mean, that the wines have been improving?

Thanks again.

posts.name: Martin Steinley
posts.cooked:
It’s generally one of the more consistent wines of the AOC, the core of the blend deriving from this parcel, which is one of the most enviably situated in the AOC

Bernard Diochon’s wines were superb and those of Thomas Patenôtre are as good, and consistent as you say. I have been drinking the wines for about 25 years and my experience is right in line with your reviews of recent vintages. I have never had an experience like that described in the OP.
